intent.one_sentence: A glass pavilion in rain with wet pavement, chrome fixtures, and warm interior light.
intent.art_goal: Make glass/refraction, chrome, puddles, rain particles, and tinted lighting readable from authored cameras.

foreground_midground_background.foreground: Puddled tile and chrome drain edge.
foreground_midground_background.midground: Glass walls, mullions, bench, and reflected warm interior.
foreground_midground_background.background: Dark garden silhouettes and cool rain haze.

lighting.key: Cool rain-blue key from above and outside.
lighting.fill: Low night fill from garden side.
lighting.rim: Warm interior fixtures through glass edges.
lighting.fog: Thin blue rain mist with warm interior scattering.

cameras.role: Table, glass accents, and wet floor as the release vignette.
cameras.art_reason: Material-led view keeps the authored table, glass/chrome accents, lantern glow, and floor reflections dominant while leaving enough distance for the tabletop props to read as small objects.

cameras.role: Glass tint/refraction with tabletop object displacement.
cameras.art_reason: Closer side view looks through the glass edge at tabletop objects instead of across the empty rear wall.

cameras.role: Wet tile, chrome, and rain reflections.
cameras.art_reason: Keeps wet floor reflection, chrome tabletop highlights, and glass edge readable without returning to the unsafe drain macro.

material_palette.description: Slight blue-gray glass with strong edge highlights and readable transmission.
Wet stone tile with puddle masks and dark grout.
Clean chrome fixtures with rain streak contrast.
Dark wet garden foliage and matte planted backdrop.
Thin water film and puddles reflecting warm/cool light.

assets.art_reason: Stable tile base anchors every glass frame and puddle.
Cooler, thinner glass is framed and mounted into the floor channel without reading as a giant colored wall.
Vertical supports and mullions give the glass a constructed pavilion frame.
A real scanned prop gives the glass something solid to frame and refract.
Small tabletop props make the close crop read as intentional material styling instead of a bare dark table.
Lower rear panels satisfy the constructed pavilion read without becoming tall procedural slabs behind the table.
Low planters break up the rear edge and keep the garden enclosure grounded.
Scanned vegetation replaces the old flat green backdrop with layered foliage silhouettes.
Round prop gives chrome a close reflection target and is checked for unsquashed scale.

authored_groups.art_reason: Frames make every glass panel read as installed architecture.
Puddles, drains, and tile seams make the rain surface physically grounded.
Rain particles and warm fixtures show transmission/reflection behavior.

seeded_random_groups.art_reason: Secondary splash detail, not scene layout.
